Figure 4 provides an overview of findings on SAV deployment for packets both outbound from and inbound to the measured network, for the year ending 1 August 2019. Deployment statistics are presented by IPv4/24 and IPv6/40 prefixes, and by AS. An AS with partial deployment originates some prefixes from which the Spoofer system did not receive spoofed packets, and originates other prefixes from which the Spoofer system did. This data indicates that while most networks block packets with spoofed source addresses, some do not. The cumulative fraction of active weeks is displayed as a function of the number of active weeks. An active week is defined as any week for which there is a test report from a client with the daemon functionality. Two failure modes are hypothesized: in the first, the NAT does not rewrite the source address because the address is not in the local network served by the NAT, leaving the spoofed source address intact to the amplifier; in the second, the NAT rewrites the source address to the NAT's publicly routable address.
